fields.helpText: This field is updated from the Audiometric Module enter/edit GUI application ACKQROES3E.EXE used in conjunction with  Remote Order Entry System 3.0 for ordering hearing aid products. Since it is crossreferenced on this field, if a previous entry already has the given date/time, it will be incremented by 1 second intervals until an unused date/time if found.

This field contains the patient for whom the audiometric data was created. It is the internal record number from the PATIENT file for the local clinic, except at the DDC.  At the DDC this field will be looked up in the DDC PATIENT file and filled in from there after transmission, from the SSN and NAME of the patient, if it can be found.

This pointer to the new person file is retrieved from the Audiometric Module Enter/Edit program.  After transmission to the DDC, the name will be looked up in the database there, and if found will be updated with the pointer to their database.

This is entered in the GUI application ACKQROES3E where it is validated and stored into this file.  After transmission to the DDC, a search of the local file is done and if a match is found, this is updated with that value.
Age of patient at time of visit
MARKED FOR DELETION The eligibility status at the time of the visit as taken from the PATIENT file. It can be PENDING VERIFICATION, PENTING RE-VERIFICATION, or VERIFIED.
The text for this field is taken from the radio buttons in the like named field in the GUI application ACKQROES3E.
Indicates if the test was performed using Ear Phones or an Insert.
This is the date the exam data is verified as accurate.
This is the Station where the exam was entered
Pointer to the Master Patient Index file
This is filled in when the data is transmitted to the DDC.
The pointer to the message that was sent to the DDC on the date in piece 12
This is the claim number of the patient at the time of the procedure. This is entered into the Audiogram Edit.
If the audiogram is retransmitted to the DDC this will track the last  date/time it was sent.
This is the user who last retransmitted the audiogram to the DDC on the  date in field .15 of the zero node.
Based on the PURE TONE thresholds taken at 1000, 2000, 3000, and 4000 Hz, for the Right ear.
This is a calculated field of the average of the readings from the 1000, 2000, 3000 and 4000 Hz fields for the Left ear.
This average is based on the threshold readings from 500, 1000 and 2000 Hz. Right ear only.
This average is based on the threshold readings from 500, 1000 and 2000 Hz. Left ear only.
This average is based on the lowest two thresholds from 500, 1000 and  2000 hz for the Right ear.
This average is based on the lowest two thresholds from 500, 1000 and 2000 hz for the Left ear.
Most Comfortable Loudness for the Right ear.
Uncomfortable Loudness for the Right ear.
Most Comfortable Loudness for the Left ear.
Uncomfortable Loudness for the Left ear.
Tympanogram Type used for the Right ear.
Tympanogram Type used for the Left ear.

The best Phonetically Balanced score obtained in a series for Right ear
The lowest Phonetically Balanced score obtained in a series for Right ear

Basically this is the PB Max minus the PB Min score, divided by the  PB Max score. But, only occurs when a second score, obtained at a higher presentation level, is poorer than a prior score at a lower presentation level.  Right ear testing. Acronym for: Performance Intensity function for Phonetically Balanced word lists
The best Phonetically Balanced score obtained in a series for Left ear
The lowest Phonetically Balanced score obtained in a series for Left ear

Basically this is the PB Max minus the PB Min score, divided by the PB Max score. But, only occurs when a second score, obtained at a higher presentation level, is poorer than a prior score at a lower presentation level.  Left ear testing.  Acronym for: Performance Intensity function for Phonetically Balanced word lists
